channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: US House backs Nigeria aid freeze over Christian persecution concerns
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The US House of Representatives has approved measures to freeze all aid to Nigeria, citing concerns over Christian persecution and the government’s failure
Read More: https://punchng.com/us-house-backs-nigeria-aid-freeze-over-christian-persecution-concerns/

item.title: Tinubu approves Lagos-Ibadan road reconstruction, legacy highway expansion
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu approves major road projects, including Lagos-Ibadan Expressway reconstruction and Fourth Legacy Highway expansion. Learn more about Niger
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-approves-lagos-ibadan-road-reconstruction-legacy-highway-expansion/

item.title: Oyo abduction: More victims discharged, fear keeps schools shut
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Fear keeps schools in Oriire LGA shut despite more Oyo abduction victims being discharged. Parents demand enhanced security before children return.
Read More: https://punchng.com/oyo-abduction-more-victims-discharged-fear-keeps-schools-shut/

item.title: REA pushes productive electricity access to drive rural enterprise
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Rural Electrification Agency (REA) is shifting focus from mere infrastructure expansion to boosting rural enterprise and agriculture through productive
Read More: https://punchng.com/rea-pushes-productive-electricity-access-to-drive-rural-enterprise/

item.title: Nurse’s death: Umahi, police fight bid to stop autopsy
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Minister David Umahi and Ebonyi Police insist on an autopsy for nurse Mary Habila, whose body was found at Umahi’s residence, despite her family’s bid to s
Read More: https://punchng.com/nurses-death-umahi-police-fight-bid-to-stop-autopsy/

item.title: LCCI Expo attracts businesses with investment opportunities
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Discover strategic partnerships and investment opportunities at the LCCI Expo. The Invest Nigeria Conference and Expo connects businesses with global marke
Read More: https://punchng.com/lcci-expo-attracts-businesses-with-investment-opportunities/

item.title: Osun poll: APC, ADC snub peace pact, Adeleke signs
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ahead of the August 15 Osun election, 12 parties signed a peace pact, but APC and ADC candidates were absent. Governor Adeleke reaffirmed commitment to a p
Read More: https://punchng.com/osun-poll-apc-adc-snub-peace-pact-adeleke-signs/

item.title: Two killed, 19 vehicles vandalised in fresh Osogbo violence
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Fresh violence in Osogbo leaves two dead and 19 vehicles vandalised. Suspected hoodlums attacked residents and a motor park in the Osun State capital.
Read More: https://punchng.com/two-killed-19-vehicles-vandalised-in-fresh-osogbo-violence/

item.title: Senate clears Fasina for ambassadorial role despite protests
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Nigerian Senate has confirmed Prof. Abayomi Fasina as a non-career ambassador, despite protests from civil society organizations over allegations again
Read More: https://punchng.com/senate-clears-fasina-for-ambassadorial-role-despite-protests/

item.title: CBN unveils centralised system to track BDC transactions
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The CBN introduces a centralised FX BDC Purchase Tracker to monitor Bureau De Change transactions in real-time, enhancing transparency and compliance in Ni
Read More: https://punchng.com/cbn-unveils-centralised-system-to-track-bdc-transactions/

item.title: Plus Acuity partners UNILAG PRAD Club to boost marketing communications skills
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Plus Acuity Nigeria Limited, a Lagos-based media strategy, planning and buying agency, has strengthened its commitment to developing the next generation of marketing communications talent through a strategic partnership with the University of Lagos Public Relations and Advertising Club for PRADWAVE

item.title: Wema Bank reinforces role in powering Fintech growth
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Wema Bank has reinforced its position as a key enabler of Nigeria’s fintech ecosystem through a strategic engagement with its fintech partners, bringing together founders, technology providers and industry stakeholders to deepen collaboration and support the continued growth of digital financial ser

item.title: Amaechi loses mother
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Chibuike Rotimi Amaechi, vice presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), has announced the passing of his beloved mother, Mrs Mary Amaechi, who departed peacefully on Thursday July 16. She was 89 years old. The post Amaechi loses mother appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Olu of Warri, Queen Consort unveil initiative to unite Itsekiri worldwide
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: His Majesty Ogiame Atuwatse III, the Olu of Warri, and Her Majesty Olori Atuwatse III, Mama Iwere, have spearheaded a cultural identity movement and digital platform known as Iam Itsekiri, aimed at uniting Itsekiri people and their friends across the world around a shared heritage and sense of belon

item.title: MTN brings ‘The Gathering on 100’ to Kano
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Thousands of young Nigerians gathered in Kano for the MTN Nigeria’s ‘The Gathering on 100’ recently, transforming the city into an immersive space for entrepreneurship, creativity, gaming, networking and entertainment. The post MTN brings ‘The Gathering on 100’ to Kano appeared first on The Sun Nige

item.title: MultiChoice announces betPawa as headline sponsor of BBNaija Season 11
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: MultiChoice has officially unveiled a sports betting platform, betPawa as the headline sponsor of Big Brother Naija Season 11, marking one of the biggest entertainment partnerships in African television. The post MultiChoice announces betPawa as headline sponsor of BBNaija Season 11 appeared first

item.title: Palo Alto Networks’ stock has been on a tear — and it could go even higher, according to these bulls
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The company’s shares have roughly doubled over the past three months, as investors have warmed to the idea that AI is increasing the need for cybersecurity products.

item.title: As SpaceX’s stock notches first close below IPO price, the company is gearing up for a crucial launch
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The megarocket’s success is SpaceX’s “single most important watch item,” one analyst says.

item.title: NYSC, reforms and quest for skills-driven national service
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: For decades, Nigerians have debated whether the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) should be reformed, fundamentally restructured or scrapped altogether. That debate may finally have found a meaningful answer. More than five decades after the scheme was established, the Federal Government has unvei

item.title: Top Currency Forecaster Says Yen May Reach 170 Against Dollar
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The most-accurate forecaster of dollar-yen in Bloomberg’s latest rankings sees the currency sliding as far as 170 against the dollar next year, using a prediction model that largely ignores the news and focuses on technical analysis instead.

item.title: Celtic struggling to compete in transfer window - CEO
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Celtic are struggling to compete with English Championship clubs in the transfer market and deal with agents, according to minutes of a meeting between board members and supporters.

item.title: The ‘Osondi Owendi’ World Cup
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The 2026 World Cup, which will end on Sunday with the final match between Spain and Argentina, has been described in so many ways by pundits, writers and commentators. Some have dubbed it the World Cup of chaos, the beautiful game and other wonderful epithets. For me, the 2026 World Cup reminds me o

item.title: Tinubu Launches $3.05bn World Bank-Backed Programmes To Fight Poverty
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Tinubu on Thursday launched five World Bank-backed programmes worth $3.05 billion aimed at accelerating poverty reduction, strengthening human capital development, improving livelihoods and building resilient communities across Nigeria. The programmes comprise the Nigeria Community Ac

item.title: Kaduna’s debt rethink in turbulent economy
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: In public finance, there comes a moment when governments must choose between political convenience and economic responsibility. One path offers the illusion of prosperity through endless borrowing; the other demands painful discipline, difficult decisions and delayed gratification. Few political lea

item.title: PFIPC Scandal: Gbajabiamila Sues Alleged Fake DG For N15bn Over Bribery Claim
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Chief of Staff to the President, Femi Gbajabiamila, has slammed a N15 billion defamation suit on the embattled Director-General of the controversial Presidential Economic Advisory Council/Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), Adeyemi Adeniyi. Gbajabiamila filed the suit in

item.title: Overcoming mental illness
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: For centuries, writers have explored human conditions in their works, blending emotional seesaws and the irregular for our reading delight and interrogation. These works offer us a mirror to see our reflections and give us a compass to navigate through the hurdles of life. Mental illness is one of t

item.title: San Francisco mayor pushes for tougher rules after the Waymo traffic fiasco
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: In the wake of a massive hours-long gridlock event, San Francisco Mayor Daniel Lurie has told state regulators its time to put more requirements on robotaxi operators like Waymo.

item.title: NEPL/Seplat Energy JV empowers over 110 youths in Edo
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Held under the theme: “Lighting Up the Nation: Empowering Youths in Renewable Energy Solutions”, the YEP graduation ceremony featured exhibitions of practical solutions developed by participants during the programme, including solar home systems, mini-grid prototypes, solar dryers, biogas digesters

item.title: Submarine Cable Disruptions Could Jeopardise 99% Of Global Internet Traffic- ITU Warns
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The International Telecommunication Union (ITU) and global telecommunications experts have called for urgent action to strengthen the resilience of submarine telecommunications cables, warning that damage to the infrastructure could disrupt financial systems, government services and internet connect

item.title: The xenophobic attack probe
item.dc:creator: The Sun Editorial
item.description: As a result of the recent xenophobic attacks on Nigerians in South Africa, the Senate has mandated its Committee on Foreign Affairs to investigate the incidents. The violent attacks came on the heels of the June 30 deadline issued by South African anti-migrant groups to foreigners to leave the count

item.title: Senate Rescinds Passage Of Proceeds Of Crime Amendment Bill For Fresh Review
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Senate on Thursday rescinded its earlier decision passing the Proceeds of Crime Act (Amendment) Bill, 2026, citing the need to address substantive drafting, legal and policy issues identified after the legislation was approved on July 9, 2026. Moving the motion, Senate Leader, Senator Opeyemi Ba

item.title: Americans say they need $1.2 million to retire. Most are too deep in debt to get there.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: More than 80% of Americans worry about running out of money in retirement: “It really keeps them up at night.”

item.title: Northern Youth Assembly Seeks Transparent Probe Into Mary Habila’s Death At Umahi’s Residence
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Northern Youth Assembly of Nigeria (NYAN) has called for an independent, transparent, and credible investigation into the death of 26-year-old Mary Habila, who reportedly died on June 27, 2026, at the private residence of the Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, in Uburu, Ohaozara Local Gover

item.title: GE boosts profit outlook, but stock falls as booming order growth cools
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: GE Aerospace’s stock falls after earnings, again, with recently rapid order-book growth slowing.

item.title: Qatar rejects Israeli reports of military action against Iran
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Qatar has rejected Israeli media reports claiming the Qataris agreed to participate in military action against Iran.

item.title: Umahi Seeks Autopsy Before Release Of Miss Habila For Burial
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Minister of Works Engr. David Umahi has requested for autopsy on late Miss Mary Habila before the release of her body for burial. The minister made the request through his legal team lead by Roy Nwaeze SAN following the death of Mary at his resident at Uburu, Ebonyi State.Miss Habila died on 27t

item.title: Sadiq Khan joins House of Lords as outgoing UK PM names 26 new peers
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: PM Keir Starmer's final honours list also includes campaigners, military leaders and senior civil servants.

item.title: Kogi High Court Denies Issuing Order Deregistering NDC
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The High Court in Kogi has dismissed as false and misleading, a social media report alleging that it ordered the deregistration of the National Democratic Congress (NDC). A statement issued by the court’s Chief Information Officer, Saqeeb Saeed, on Thursday in Lokoja, the state capital, said it neit

item.title: SpaceX suddenly aborts second Starship V3 launch after ignition
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The company didn't immediately say what went wrong. SpaceX's stock plunged more than 4% in after-hours trading before paring losses.

item.title: Nigeria, The Gambia Agree To Revive Joint Commission, Expand Bilateral Cooperation
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria and The Gambia have reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening diplomatic relations, with both countries agreeing to revive the Nigeria-Gambia Joint Commission to enhance cooperation in areas of mutual interest. The resolution was reached during a bilateral meeting between Nigeria’s Minist

item.title: French MPs Approve Assisted Dying Bill Amid Strict Conditions After Years of Debate
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: France’s National Assembly has approved a landmark bill to legalise assisted dying under strict conditions, marking a significant step in the country’s long-running debate over end-of-life care. Lawmakers voted 291 to 241 in favour of the legislation, which seeks to grant eligible adults with termin

item.title: Delta Youths Petitions National Assembly Over Alleged Fraud In NDDC internship Programme
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Anioma Youth Forum Worldwide has petitioned the Senate and the House of Representatives over alleged irregularities in the Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) Youth Internship Programme, calling for a comprehensive investigation into its implementation between August 2024 and July 2025. Th

item.title: Netflix is getting stingier about its viewing data, and Wall Street isn’t happy
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Netflix’s stock is falling in the wake of mixed earnings and a new plan to cut back on the publication of ‘What We Watched’ reports.

item.title: Trump tightens US visa rules, limits stay for foreign students and journalists
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration on Thursday finalized rules imposing stricter limits on how long foreign students and journalists may stay in the United States, tightening legal immigration. Student visas would be capped at four years, while journalists would be limited to 240-day stays. Chi

item.title: My car's OTA update broke Android Auto, and it's a indictment of modern software
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://imdanielkendall.com/the-great-software-regress-how-move-fast-and-break-things-broke-our-lives/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48941129 Points: 76 # Comments: 69

item.title: Show HN: Mojibake – a low-level Unicode library written in C
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: I've written Mojibake because I don't like the other Unicode libraries for Unicode support.It consists of only two amalgamation files: mojibake.h and mojibake.c. I've added all the most important Unicode algorithms, such as normalization, case conversion, segmentation, bidirectional text, collation,

item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Inter Milan work on deals for Djed Spence and Cristian Romero, Liverpool lead the race for Kaishu Sano, Arsenal show interest in Ezri Konsa, plus more.

item.title: US forces board ship to ensure ‘compliance’ with Iran blockade
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The military command also said Thursday it had "redirected 3 commercial vessels trying to run the blockade" since it took effect at 2000 GMT on Tuesday. The post US forces board ship to ensure ‘compliance’ with Iran blockade appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: 'England can learn from phenomenal Root' - but are they doing so?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Joe Root bails England out of trouble. Sound familiar?

item.title: NBA to proceed with Saturday’s election, says SSS has released detained service provider’s MD
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: NBA said its NEC affirmed that there was no operational or logistical reason to postpone the election, now two days away. The post NBA to proceed with Saturday’s election, says SSS has released detained service provider’s MD appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Intuitive Surgical Drops as ACA Changes, GLP-1s Weigh on Growth
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Intuitive Surgical shares fell as much as 13% in after-market trading as growth in use of its da Vinci surgical robots slowed to the weakest pace in four years and its guidance for procedure growth was unchanged.

item.title: Bank Rally Hits Short Sellers Betting on Australia Lending Slump
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The upward march in share prices of Australia’s biggest banks is reversing the tumble sparked two months ago by property tax changes, and inflicting pain on short sellers who were anticipating a prolonged plunge.

item.title: At least one dead in Texas floods ravaging same area where campers died
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The same area devastated by floods last year's floods, which caused more than 130 deaths, is once again being hit.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Stocks in Asia looked set to open lower Friday after a selloff in chipmakers dragged down Wall Street as investors questioned whether massive artificial-intelligence investments can justify lofty valuations.

item.title: Former Italian motorway chief sentenced over bridge collapse
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The former CEO of Italy’s main highway operator has been given 12 years in prison.

item.title: US strikes in Iran hit airport, bridges, railway station
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Deadly US strikes overnight hit an airport, a railway station in the port city of Bandar Abbas and two bridges in southern Iran near the Strait of Hormuz, Iranian state media said Friday. The post US strikes in Iran hit airport, bridges, railway station appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil extended a weekly advance as the escalating conflict between the US and Iran raised concerns about disruptions to supply from the Middle East.

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: A security team comprising operatives of Department of State Security (DSS) and forest guards on Thursday rescued two victims of abduction in a forest between Oba Isin/Owa Onire area of isin/ Ifelodun Local Government Areas of Kwara State. The rescued children are 16-year-old Fatima Azeez and Ibrahi

item.description: The resolution comes days after US Secretary of State Marco Rubio pledged to 'dismantle' the international court.

item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Nigeria Society of Physiotherapy (NSP) has said that the name of Mary Habila, the young woman who died at the residence of Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi in Ebonyi State, does not appear in the database of registered physiotherapists. This contradicts claims by the minister’s team

item.title: Tinubu presents Shettima certificate as APC Vice-Presidential candidate for 2027
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has officially presented Vice President Kashim Shettima with a certificate confirming his nomination as the All Progressives Congress (APC) vice-presidential candidate for the 2027 presidential election. The presentation followed the APC’s confirmation of Shettima as Tinubu’s r

item.title: Pele’s World Cup jersey fetches $4.9 million at US auction
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Brazilian attacker nicknamed "The King" was 17 when he scored two goals in his country's 5-2 victory over host country Sweden in 1958. The post Pele’s World Cup jersey fetches $4.9 million at US auction appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: ‘They are relentless’: A scammer is trying to change the email on my empty Robinhood account. What do they have to gain?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I’ve just been ignoring their attempts.”

item.title: How the Iran War Is Rewiring the Oil Market
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The US-Israel war with Iran was supposed to blow up energy markets, but that hasn’t happened yet. In this weekly documentary, Bloomberg Originals explains why.

item.title: UN finds looted gold, gum arabic bankrolling Sudan war
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Proceeds from looted gold and gum arabic are being used by warring parties in Sudan to fund the war, the UN human rights office has verified. A new OHCHR report described gum arabic and gold sales as “a major revenue source” for the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and paramilitary Rapid Supp

item.title: Canadian wildfire sends hazardous smoke spewing into US
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: FRANCE 24's Yinka Oyetade speaks with Jonathan Boucher, Researcher at the Canadian Forest Service, about the measures needed to manage and prevent wildfires and render forests more resilient to the ravages of climate change. Ontario requested federal assistance to evacuate people from remote norther

item.title: US Limits Length Of Visas For Students, Journalists
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Under a change that could be implemented as soon as September, foreigners on student visas would be admitted for the length of their academic programme up to a maximum of four years. The post US Limits Length Of Visas For Students, Journalists appeared first on Channels Television.
